articles.title: Bus Route Changes Improve Weekend Service
articles.summary: The transit agency is rolling out new weekend schedules to make public transportation more frequent and reliable for residents.
articles.content: Starting this month, the Riverton Transit Authority is increasing weekend bus frequency on several popular routes. Public transportation riders will see shorter wait times on Saturdays and Sundays, especially between downtown and the east side. Officials say the goal is to make it easier for workers, students, and families to rely on buses instead of cars. The changes come after a year-long study of ridership patterns and public feedback sessions held across the city.

articles.title: New Express Train Links Downtown and Riverton
articles.summary: A 25-minute express rail service now connects Riverton Station to downtown, expanding public transportation options for commuters.
articles.content: Commuters who rely on public transportation have a faster option starting today. The new express train between Riverton Station and downtown Central City cuts travel time nearly in half, with limited stops and upgraded onboard Wi-Fi. City leaders hope the line will reduce traffic on the highway and encourage more people to leave their cars at home. Officials are monitoring ridership closely during the first 90 days to determine if additional trains should be added during peak hours.

articles.title: How Seniors Use Public Transportation to Stay Connected
articles.summary: Local seniors share how buses and trains help them reach medical appointments, social events, and community programs.
articles.content: For many older adults in Riverton, public transportation is more than a convenience—it is a lifeline. Seniors interviewed for this story described how reliable buses, trains, and paratransit services help them attend medical appointments, visit family members, and participate in community centers. Advocates say continued investment in accessible stops, benches, and clear signage will make the system easier to navigate for everyone.

articles.title: City Pilots On-Demand Shuttles for Late-Night Workers
articles.summary: A new pilot program uses app-based booking to extend public transportation access past midnight for service industry employees.
articles.content: Riverton has launched a six-month pilot of on-demand shuttles that operate after regular bus service ends. The service focuses on restaurant, hospital, and retail workers who rely on public transportation but often finish shifts after midnight. Riders can request a shuttle through a mobile app or by phone. City officials say the pilot will help them understand demand for late-night service and could inform future changes to fixed bus routes.

articles.title: City Council Approves New Park
articles.summary: After months of debate, the council voted to turn a vacant lot into a neighborhood park with trails, playgrounds, and bike lanes.
articles.content: In a unanimous vote last night, the city council approved plans for a new neighborhood park on the site of a long-vacant lot near Oak Street. The design includes a playground, community garden beds, and a loop trail that connects to existing bike lanes along the river. Supporters say the project will bring much-needed green space to nearby apartments and encourage walking and cycling. Construction is expected to begin next spring, pending final environmental review.

articles.title: Top 10 Patio Brunch Spots for Spring
articles.summary: From river views to hidden courtyards, these local restaurants make the most of outdoor dining season.
articles.content: As the weather warms up, Riverton locals flock to patios for weekend brunch. We rounded up ten spots with standout menus, sunny seating, and kid-friendly options. Whether you prefer bottomless coffee, classic waffles, or creative vegan dishes, there is a patio table waiting for you.

articles.title: Local Artist Transforms Alleyways Into Open-Air Galleries
articles.summary: A series of murals is turning overlooked spaces into colorful community landmarks.
articles.content: A Riverton painter is teaming up with building owners to create large-scale murals in alleyways across downtown. The project aims to brighten neglected spaces while celebrating neighborhood history and culture. Residents can follow a self-guided walking tour map to see each new piece as it is completed.

articles.title: Five Ways to Save on Your Next Grocery Trip
articles.summary: With food prices rising, small changes in how you shop can add up to big savings.
articles.content: From planning meals around sales to joining local co-ops, Riverton residents are finding creative ways to stretch their grocery budgets. We spoke with financial counselors and shoppers about strategies that really work.

articles.title: Renter’s Guide to Navigating Rising Housing Costs
articles.summary: Experts share tips on negotiating leases, finding roommates, and spotting scams in a competitive rental market.
articles.content: As rents rise across the region, many Riverton tenants are struggling to keep up. Housing counselors recommend preparing for lease renewals early, understanding your rights, and considering shared housing to reduce costs.

articles.title: Community Garden Sign-Ups Open for Spring Season
articles.summary: Plots at Riverton’s three community gardens go quickly—here’s how to apply.
articles.content: Garden coordinators encourage interested residents to apply early, as plots are assigned on a first-come, first-served basis. Volunteers are also needed for maintenance days and youth gardening workshops.

articles.title: Behind the Scenes at Riverton Community Theater
articles.summary: Volunteers juggle costumes, lights, and last-minute line changes in this season’s biggest production.
articles.content: From building sets to running the sound board, community theater volunteers keep the show running. We spent a week backstage to see what it takes to bring a production to life.

articles.title: Draft: Neighborhood Voices on Downtown Redevelopment
articles.summary: Unpublished draft collecting resident feedback about proposed zoning changes.
articles.content: Editors are still collecting quotes and fact-checking details for this story about downtown redevelopment.

deals.title: 30% Off Whole Bean Coffee at Cornerstone
deals.description: Save 30% on any 12 oz or larger bag of whole bean coffee roasted in-house at Cornerstone Coffee Roasters.
deals.terms: Valid in-store only at the Riverton location. Limit 3 bags per customer. Cannot be combined with other offers. Must present code at checkout.

deals.title: 25% Off Any Drink at Green Leaf Cafe
deals.description: Enjoy 25% off any handcrafted coffee, tea, or specialty drink at Green Leaf Cafe.
deals.terms: Valid once per customer per day. Dine-in or takeout. Excludes bottled beverages and retail coffee beans.

deals.title: 20% Off Coffee & Pastry Combo at Café on the Green
deals.description: Get 20% off when you order a brewed coffee or latte with any pastry before 11 a.m.
deals.terms: Valid weekdays only before 11 a.m. One combo per customer per visit. Not valid with senior discount.

deals.title: 20% Off Dinner Entrées at Riverton Noodle House
deals.description: Take 20% off any dinner entrée after 5 p.m. at Riverton Noodle House.
deals.terms: Valid for dine-in only after 5 p.m. Excludes lunch specials and takeout orders. Gratuity not included.

deals.title: 15% Off Family Meal at Sunrise Diner
deals.description: Save 15% on your total bill when you order a family meal combo for four at Sunrise Diner.
deals.terms: Valid for dine-in only. Minimum purchase of one family meal combo. Not valid with other discounts or on holidays.

deals.title: 40% Off Weekday Matinee Movie Tickets
deals.description: Enjoy 40% off regular-priced adult tickets for weekday matinee showtimes at Riverton Cinema.
deals.terms: Valid Monday–Friday for shows starting before 4 p.m. Not valid for special events or 3D screenings.

deals.title: BOGO Free Yoga Class at Riverfront Yoga Studio
deals.description: Buy one drop-in class and get one free for a friend at Riverfront Yoga Studio.
deals.terms: First-time students only. Advance registration required. Free class must be used on the same day.

deals.title: 10% Off at Riverton Artisan Market
deals.description: Take 10% off your entire purchase from participating vendors at the Riverton Artisan Market.
deals.terms: Valid only at booths displaying the promo sign. One discount per transaction. Not valid on gift cards.

deals.title: Free Dessert with Two Entrées at La Plaza Taqueria
deals.description: Order any two dinner entrées and receive one dessert of equal or lesser value free.
deals.terms: Dine-in only after 4 p.m. One free dessert per table. Not valid with Taco Tuesday specials.

deals.title: 50% Off Cold Brew Growlers Next Month
deals.description: Get 50% off 64 oz cold brew growler fills every Monday next month at Cornerstone Coffee Roasters.
deals.terms: Valid Mondays only during the promotional period. Customer must provide their own growler. Not valid on bottled cold brew.

deals.title: Student 20% Off Lunch at Seasons Vegan Kitchen
deals.description: Show a valid student ID to receive 20% off any lunch entrée at Seasons Vegan Kitchen.
deals.terms: Valid weekdays from 11 a.m. to 3 p.m. Dine-in only. Must present a current student ID.

deals.title: Holiday Coffee Bundle 25% Off (Expired)
deals.description: Save 25% on a holiday bundle including a 12 oz bag of coffee and a mug at Cornerstone Coffee Roasters.
deals.terms: Valid on pre-packaged holiday bundles while supplies lasted. Not combinable with other promotions.
